approach: Close the authoritative McKay order-42 corpus gate, then strengthen its actual-record parser audit after acquisition remained blocked

strategy.mechanism: A frozen byte preflight gates all mathematics; passing bytes must then agree under two exact forbidden-set evaluators, a third NetworkX graph6 signature path, nauty complementation, and canonical labeling.

hypothesis: The authoritative McKay corpus is retrievable unchanged and, if admitted by the frozen byte discriminator, can be audited through a third independent parser without weakening the fail-closed source gate.
discriminating_test: Attempt one bounded authoritative download; require the frozen 47,888-byte SHA-256 before graph work; in parallel require the new NetworkX signature path to agree with both checker parsers on every order-7 isomorphism class and complement.
summary: The authoritative curl acquisition failed DNS resolution with return code 6 after one retry and left no corpus. The target preflight therefore again returned code 2 with downstream_checks_run=false, so no order-42 graph or K43 repair neighborhood was checked. The corpus gate was strengthened to require NetworkX order/edge/ordered-degree signatures on every admitted source record and complement. The new path passed an exhaustive 1,044-class order-7 regression (2,088 records including complements), with exact checker-output agreement and byte-exact nauty complementation. Current literature still gives 43 <= R(5,5) <= 46; arXiv:2508.16699 was rejected as status-changing because it explicitly supplies a numerical/prime-sequence heuristic estimate rather than a witness or exclusion proof.
rationale: The only positive result is verification infrastructure: a new independent parser obligation was implemented and exhaustively attacked on the smallest suite sharing the order-42 padding pattern. The absent authoritative bytes prevent any claim about the supplied 328 records, their complements, novelty, or R(5,5).
claims: The target corpus gate now contains a third NetworkX graph6 signature check for every admitted source record and complement.
That exact signature path agrees with both checker parsers on all 1,044 unlabeled order-7 graph representatives and all 1,044 complements; local complement bytes agree with nauty-complg.
The authoritative order-42 corpus remained absent, so zero order-42 corpus records were checked this epoch.
The maintained rigorous range remains 43 <= R(5,5) <= 46.
next_steps: Acquire unchanged authoritative bytes at sources/r55_42some.g6 with final URL, timestamp, headers, size, and SHA-256.
If and only if the byte preflight matches, run the enhanced corpus command in CHECKPOINT.md and require two exact evaluators plus the third NetworkX signature path on all 656 controls.
After the corpus passes, authenticate Springer Supplementary Data 4 before any canonical comparison or K43 repair search.
Do not repeat command-environment acquisition without a new network path or user-provided raw bytes.
experiments: .proof-experiments/20260720-200524-16f6f3: authoritative acquisition failed with curl code 6; no source file remained
.proof-experiments/20260720-200920-965ada: first third-parser regression passed but was superseded because the imported corpus-gate source hash was absent from the report
.proof-experiments/20260720-200957-c4fca8: corrected third-parser regression passed on 2,088 order-7 source/complement records
.proof-experiments/20260720-201015-92e71b: strengthened gate still failed closed on absent source with downstream_checks_run=false
independent_checker: Checker A is Python with direct C(n,5) enumeration and an independent graph6 parser; Checker B is C with a separate parser and recursive bitset clique enumeration. NetworkX 3.3 is a third decoder used only for order/edge/ordered-degree signatures, while nauty-complg independently checks complement bytes. The new path has not run on order 42 because the source is absent.
transfer_insights: Isolated adversarial reconstruction -> an external parser should detect a shared custom graph6 error -> NetworkX agreed on all 2,088 order-7 source/complement controls.
Fail-closed data provenance -> missing bytes must prevent downstream mathematics -> the strengthened gate returned source_preflight_failed with downstream_checks_run=false.

established_facts.claim: The NetworkX signature path embedded in the corpus gate agrees with both exact checker parsers and nauty complementation on every unlabeled order-7 graph class and its complement.
established_facts.scope: 1,044 source representatives plus 1,044 complements at order 7 only

established_facts.claim: The maintained published range remains 43 <= R(5,5) <= 46.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Start order-42 novelty or K43 repair search from the current local packet.
ruled_out.scope: Any continuation without the authoritative McKay corpus and publisher supplementary seed
ruled_out.reason: The byte gate failed before every target-instance check.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: The authoritative corpus passes the enhanced two-evaluator/three-parser control gate.

ruled_out.reason: The paper describes randomized numerical diagnostics and a prime-sequence heuristic estimate, not a valid witness or exhaustive exclusion.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Supply an explicit independently checked witness or a complete proof/certificate satisfying the verification contract.

open_leads.description: Authenticate the 328 McKay records and 328 complements under the enhanced gate.
open_leads.rationale: Every novelty, deletion, and canonical-comparison claim depends on these immutable controls.
open_leads.next_experiment: Place unchanged bytes at sources/r55_42some.g6 and run the exact command in CHECKPOINT.md.

open_leads.description: Authenticate Springer Supplementary Data 4 after the corpus gate passes.
open_leads.rationale: The published two-conflict K43 is the strongest bounded repair seed, but the local transcription has no publisher provenance.
open_leads.next_experiment: Retrieve the unchanged TXT, hash it, decode both color conventions, and require exact dual-checker violation identities.

blocker: The managed command environment cannot resolve the authoritative host, the browser interface cannot export the raw body, and no authenticated corpus exists locally.
reopen_condition: Provide or retrieve unchanged McKay bytes with provenance; they must have 47,888 bytes, 328 LF-terminated records, and SHA-256 067902e853d87b49bcef0d1d4c0e3bbadd238ee18bc65341b079a3ca4780eccb before downstream checks run.

continuation.objective: Close the authoritative order-42 corpus control gate.
continuation.first_action: Acquire sources/r55_42some.g6 through a newly available network path or user-provided unchanged file, then run the r55-authenticated-corpus-gate command in CHECKPOINT.md.
continuation.stop_condition: Stop at any byte hash, record count, exact checker, NetworkX signature, forbidden-set, histogram, degree, complement, or canonical-label mismatch; otherwise proceed to the Springer source gate.

candidate_profile.scholarly_question: Can computational R(5,5) experiments be based on a source-authenticated control corpus with mutually independent parsing and exact evaluation paths?
candidate_profile.meaningful_delta: The existing gate had two exact evaluators but its third parser was tested only in a separate plumbing script; epoch 2 made the NetworkX signature a mandatory per-record obligation of the eventual target corpus gate and recorded its imported source hash.
candidate_profile.acceptance_test: The unchanged authoritative 328-record corpus and all complements pass the enhanced gate from a cold start, followed by an independent external replay.

closest_prior_work.difference: McKay supplies the historical graphs; this artifact adds fail-closed independent verification plumbing and claims no new graph or classification.

novelty_searches.source: Dynamic Survey DS1.18 and targeted web/arXiv search
novelty_searches.finding: No rigorous result beyond 43 <= R(5,5) <= 46 was located; the apparent R(5,5)=45 item is explicitly heuristic.

external_channel.acceptance_path: No contact or submission until the authoritative raw-source replay succeeds.

independent_validations.result: Exact complement-byte and graph-signature agreement on every order-7 isomorphism class and complement

tool_disclosure: GPT-5.6 Sol acted as principal investigator. Two precomputed GPT-5.6 Terra memos supplied advisory literature and verification reconnaissance; Sol verified their SHA-256 hashes and independently checked every relied-on source/artifact. Deterministic tools were Python 3.12.3, C/GCC, nauty 2.8.8+ds-5, NetworkX 3.3, curl, SHA-256, Git diagnostics, the computational-researcher experiment harness, and web search. No new subagent, SAT solver, proof assistant, or cloud job was used.
